frameworks.name: optimal evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: exact computation of optimal inference from discrete evidence pulses

frameworks.name: leaky integration / evidence discounting
frameworks.one_line: timescale of evidence integration adapted to environmental volatility

frameworks.name: signal-detection with sensory noise
frameworks.one_line: sensory noise enters as a component of optimal timescale

core_question: Do rats adopt the optimal timescale for discounting old evidence in a dynamic environment?
key_assumptions: discrete evidence pulses permit exact computation of optimal inference
optimal timescale depends jointly on stimulus statistics and sensory noise
world state can change within a trial, making old evidence non-informative
key_findings: rats discount old evidence at the optimal timescale when both stimulus statistics and sensory noise are considered
experimentally manipulating volatility shifts rats' accumulation timescale as predicted
interpretations: rats perform near-optimal inference in dynamic environments, unlike previously studied humans
controlling accumulation timescale via volatility opens new mechanistic investigation avenues
